name: Chocolate Pudding Cake with Bitterswseet Chocolate Sauce-
recipeInstructions:
1. Day before serving, prepare and assemble Chocolate Cake and Pudding.
- Prepare Chocolate Cake: Grease a 10-inch springform pan; sprinkle inside with 2 tablespoons sugar. Sift flour, cocoa, and baking powder together in a medium bowl.
- Heat oven to 350°F. Beat eggs and remaining 3/4 cup sugar until double in volume (5 minutes). Fold the flour mixture into the egg mixture until combined. Pour batter into prepared pan and bake for 20-25 minutes or until center springs back when gently pressed with a fingertip.
- Cool cake on wire rack for 10 minutes. Remove from pan and cool completely.
2. Prepare Pudding: In a saucepan, heat cream to boiling. Whisk egg yolks and sugar in a bowl until well combined. Slowly beat hot cream into yolk mixture and pour back into saucepan. Cook over low heat until pudding coats a spoon (do not boil). Stir in chocolate chips until smooth. Cool to room temperature.
3. Assemble Chocolate Pudding Cake: Cut cake horizontally into 3 equal slices. Line the springform pan with aluminum foil. Place top layer of cake in bottom of pan. Pour one-third of pudding over cake layer. Top with middle cake layer and half of remaining pudding. Place bottom cake layer, bottom side up, in pan and top with remaining pudding. Cover loosely and refrigerate overnight.
4. Bake Chocolate Pudding Cake: Preheat oven to 300°F. Place springform pan in a large roasting pan. Pour enough boiling water into the roasting pan to come halfway up the side of the springform pan. Bake for 1 hour or until top is glazed over.
5. Cool cake on wire rack for 15 minutes. Cover loosely and refrigerate until well chilled.
6. Prepare Sauce: Heat cream to boiling in a saucepan. Remove from heat and stir in chocolate and Grand Marnier until smooth. Strain sauce and keep warm. If desired, prepare Chocolate Curls.
7. Serve by removing side of springform pan and slicing cake into wedges. Place each wedge on a dessert plate. Top with chocolate sauce and Chocolate Curls, if desired.
Making Chocolate Curls:
- Leave a block or square of white or semisweet chocolate at room temperature (80-85°F) for several hours or heat in the microwave oven 7-8 seconds until softened slightly. Place softened chocolate against a paper towel; using a sharp vegetable peeler, draw the blade into the edge or side of the chocolate to create curls. If chocolate is too cold, it will splinter; if too warm, it will not curl.
